original_hypothesis.title: VRF callbackGasLimit Default (40000) Insufficient for setTokenHash Storage Write, Permanently Bricking Token Hashes
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: NextGenRandomizerVRF sets callbackGasLimit to 40000 as a state variable default. The fulfillRandomWords callback must execute gencoreContract.setTokenHash() which performs: 2x cold SLOAD (~4200 gas) + 1x SSTORE zero-to-nonzero (~22100 gas) + external CALL overhead (~2600 gas) + event emission (~1500 gas). Total exceeds 30,000 gas just for setTokenHash, plus fulfillRandomWords overhead. If total exceeds 40,000, the VRF coordinator's callback reverts (out of gas), the token hash is never set, LINK is consumed, and the token is permanently stuck in 'pending' metadata state.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The default callbackGasLimit of 40000 is demonstrably too low for the operations performed in fulfillRandomWords. This is a code defect in the default parameter that would affect all VRF-based collections unless an admin proactively increases the gas limit.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: RandomizerVRF.sol line 17: uint32 public callbackGasLimit = 40000; this is the default code value, not a deployment parameter

proof_of_signal.pattern: callbackGasLimit = 40000 default; fulfillRandomWords calls gencoreContract.setTokenHash which requires ~32000+ gas for cold storage operations alone

preconditions: Admin has not updated callbackGasLimit above default
VRF coordinator enforces gas limit on callback execution

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: setTokenHash: 2*SLOAD_COLD(2100) + SSTORE_ZERO_TO_NONZERO(22100) + CALL(2600) + overhead ≈ 31000+ gas. Plus fulfillRandomWords overhead ≈ 4000-6000 gas. Total ≈ 35000-37000+, close to or exceeding 40000
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: No attacker needed - this is a code defect. All VRF-based mints fail to receive hashes
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: LINK subscription funds are consumed without successful fulfillment; tokens are permanently stuck without metadata

economic_questions: What is the exact gas cost of fulfillRandomWords with cold storage access?
Does Chainlink VRF v2 consume subscription LINK even on failed callbacks?
How many collections use VRF vs NXT randomizer?
validation_requirements: Deploy with default callbackGasLimit and attempt VRF fulfillment
Measure actual gas consumed by fulfillRandomWords
Verify if failed callback can be retried

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: LINK consumed per failed VRF request
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A - DoS impact on all VRF collections

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: All VRF-based tokens have permanently broken metadata

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Token stuck in 'pending' state, potentially worthless
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire value of all VRF-based collection mints

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Exact gas profiling of fulfillRandomWords with cold SLOAD/SSTORE costs
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare 40000 limit vs actual gas consumption on fork test

validation_result.impact_summary: The default callbackGasLimit of 40000 in RandomizerVRF.sol is insufficient for the fulfillRandomWords callback which must execute setTokenHash (containing a 22100-gas SSTORE plus multiple cold SLOADs and an external CALL). Failed callbacks permanently brick token hashes with no retry mechanism.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The fulfillRandomWords callback in RandomizerVRF.sol must perform the following operations within the callbackGasLimit budget of 40000 gas:

1. rawFulfillRandomWords entry (VRFConsumerBaseV2): cold SLOAD of vrfCoordinator for msg.sender check = 2100 gas
2. fulfillRandomWords: cold SLOAD requestToToken[_requestId] = 2100 gas
3. Cold SLOAD tokenIdToCollection[...] = 2100 gas
4. Cold SLOAD gencoreContract address = 2100 gas
5. abi.encodePacked computation = ~300 gas
6. External CALL to gencoreContract (cold account) = 2600 gas
7. Inside setTokenHash: cold SLOAD collectionAdditionalData[_collectionID].randomizerContract = 2100 gas
8. Cold SLOAD tokenToHash[_mintIndex] = 2100 gas
9. SSTORE tokenToHash[_mintIndex] zero-to-nonzero = 22100 gas
10. Event emission RequestFulfilled = ~1774 gas
11. Various opcodes overhead = ~1000 gas

Conservative total: ~40374 gas, which exceeds the 40000 limit. The SSTORE zero-to-nonzero (22100) alone consumes 55% of the budget. With 5 cold SLOADs (10500), CALL overhead (2600), event (1774), and VRFConsumerBaseV2 overhead (2100+), the total is reliably above 40000.

When the callback runs out of gas, Chainlink VRF v2 considers the request fulfilled (proof was verified). LINK is consumed. There is no retry mechanism - the requestId is spent. The token hash remains 0x0 permanently. The tokenURI returns 'pending' metadata forever. There is no admin function to manually set a hash or retry a VRF request for an already-minted token.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is verified: RandomizerVRF.sol line 17 sets `uint32 public callbackGasLimit = 40000;` and lines 60-63 show fulfillRandomWords calling gencoreContract.setTokenHash() which performs a zero-to-nonzero SSTORE (22100 gas) plus multiple cold storage reads. NextGenCore.sol setTokenHash function at lines ~299-302 confirms the SSTORE and two require checks involving cold SLOADs.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Default value of 40000 is insufficient for the operations in fulfillRandomWords. The SSTORE zero-to-nonzero in setTokenHash alone costs 22100 gas, leaving only ~17900 for all other operations (5+ cold SLOADs, external CALL, event emission, VRFConsumerBaseV2 overhead).

This function performs multiple cold SLOADs (requestToToken, tokenIdToCollection, gencoreContract address), an external CALL to a cold address (gencoreContract), and emits an event. All within the 40000 gas budget which is consumed primarily by setTokenHash's SSTORE.

Contains 2 cold SLOADs (collectionAdditionalData mapping and tokenToHash mapping) at 2100 gas each, plus a zero-to-nonzero SSTORE at 22100 gas. Total for this function alone: ~26400 gas, leaving only ~13600 for all other callback operations.

calling_sequence: 1. Chainlink VRF Coordinator calls rawFulfillRandomWords on NextGenRandomizerVRF with callbackGasLimit=40000
2. rawFulfillRandomWords (VRFConsumerBaseV2) verifies msg.sender == vrfCoordinator (cold SLOAD: 2100 gas)
7. External CALL to gencoreContract.setTokenHash (cold account: 2600, address SLOAD: 2100)
12. Total: ~39374-41000+ gas — exceeds or borderline exceeds 40000 limit
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The VRF coordinator enforces the callbackGasLimit when invoking rawFulfillRandomWords. With a budget of 40000 gas and minimum required operations totaling ~39000-41000 gas (dominated by 22100 for SSTORE and 10500+ for cold SLOADs), the callback is at extreme risk of out-of-gas revert. Any additional overhead from ABI decoding, memory expansion, or slightly different storage layout pushes it over the limit. On failure, LINK is consumed, the token hash remains 0x0 permanently, and there is no retry mechanism.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Default callbackGasLimit value is hardcoded in contract source as 40000
Deployment scripts use NXT randomizer, not VRF - but VRF contract exists for production use
config_validation.deployment_evidence: fixturesDeployment.js deploys NextGenRandomizerNXT, not NextGenRandomizerVRF. However, RandomizerVRF.sol is part of the codebase and intended for production VRF-based collections. The default value of 40000 is in the source code, not a deployment parameter.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an active attack but a code defect. Any collection that uses the VRF randomizer without an admin first calling updatecallbackGasLimitAndkeyHash to increase the gas limit will have all minted tokens permanently stuck without hashes. The sequence is: (1) Collection created with VRF randomizer, (2) User mints token, (3) calculateTokenHash triggers VRF request with 40000 gas callback, (4) VRF coordinator delivers randomness, callback runs out of gas, (5) LINK consumed, hash never set, (6) Token permanently shows 'pending' metadata. No admin intervention can fix already-affected tokens since there's no retry function.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. All tokens minted on VRF-based collections using the default callbackGasLimit will have permanently broken metadata (tokenURI returns 'pending'). LINK subscription funds are consumed without successful delivery. There is no mechanism to retry failed VRF callbacks or manually set token hashes for affected tokens. The admin function updatecallbackGasLimitAndkeyHash can prevent future occurrences but cannot fix already-bricked tokens.
recommended_mitigations: Increase default callbackGasLimit to at least 100000 (or preferably 200000) to safely accommodate all operations in fulfillRandomWords including setTokenHash's SSTORE
Add an admin function to manually set token hashes for tokens where VRF callback failed (emergency recovery)
Add a retry mechanism that allows re-requesting VRF randomness for tokens with 0x0 hash
Consider adding a gas estimation test that verifies fulfillRandomWords succeeds within the configured callbackGasLimit
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full system with VRF randomizer using default callbackGasLimit=40000. 2. Fork mainnet with Chainlink VRF coordinator. 3. Create collection and assign VRF randomizer. 4. Mint a token to trigger calculateTokenHash -> requestRandomWords. 5. Simulate VRF fulfillment with gas limit of 40000. 6. Observe out-of-gas revert. 7. Verify token hash remains 0x0. 8. Verify tokenURI returns 'pending'. 9. Measure exact gas consumption of successful fulfillment (with higher gas limit) to confirm the deficit.
uncertainties: Exact gas costs depend on Solidity optimizer output and EVM implementation specifics (different clients may vary slightly)

The estimate is borderline (39000-41000 range) — in some specific execution contexts with warm storage slots from prior operations, it MIGHT barely succeed, but cold storage access in a fresh callback context makes failure highly likely
Chainlink VRF v2 coordinator's exact behavior on callback failure (whether LINK is fully consumed or partially refunded) depends on the coordinator version
additional_testing_needed: Exact gas profiling of fulfillRandomWords with callbackGasLimit=40000 on a mainnet fork
Verification of VRF coordinator behavior when callback reverts (LINK consumption, retry possibility)
Testing with callbackGasLimit values from 40000 to 100000 to find the minimum viable limit
